meta.name: Analyze support screenshots with UploadToURL, GPT-4o Vision, Zendesk, and Jira

parameters.content:
Analyze support screenshots with UploadToURL, OpenAI Vision, and Zendesk/Jira
The Problem: Large image attachments clog support inboxes and often reach developers without technical context, leading to slow resolution times.
The Solution: A support-to-dev pipeline that hosts visual proof via UploadToURL, uses AI Vision to transcribe errors, and syncs the data to Zendesk or Jira.

⚙️ How it Works
Webhook: Receives a screenshot/video (Binary or URL) and Ticket ID.

UploadToURL: Hosts the file instantly and returns a public CDN link.

GPT-4o Vision: Analyzes the image to identify error messages and UI states.

Ticket Update: Attaches the link and AI analysis to the relevant Zendesk or Jira ticket.

🔐 Credentials & Setup
Node: Install n8n-nodes-uploadtourl via Community Nodes.

APIs: UploadToURL, OpenAI (Vision), and Zendesk/Jira.

Variables: Set ZENDESK_SUBDOMAIN or JIRA_BASE_URL.

## 🚪 Entry, Validation & Upload
**Nodes:** Webhook → Validate & Enrich → Has Remote URL? → Upload to URL (×2) → Extract CDN URL

- Accepts `POST` with screenshot/video + ticket metadata. Supports both `fileUrl` (remote) and binary multipart upload
- Validates ticket ID format per platform (Zendesk: numeric, Jira: `PROJECT-123` pattern), sanitises all string inputs
- Detects file type from extension — allowlist: `png`, `jpg`, `jpeg`, `gif`, `webp`, `mp4`, `mov`; rejects all others with `400`
- Native **Upload to URL** node handles both paths — no custom HTTP node needed
- `Extract CDN URL` normalises the response shape and force-upgrades to HTTPS

## 🤖 GPT-4o Vision Analysis
**Nodes:** GPT-4o Vision → Parse AI Analysis → Determine Severity

- Sends the hosted CDN image URL directly to GPT-4o Vision — no base64 encoding, no file passing
- Prompt instructs the model to return structured JSON: `errorSummary`, `visibleErrorMessage`, `affectedComponent`, `browserOrOS`, `reproducibilityHint`, `developerNotes`, `suggestedPriority`, `detectedKeywords[]`, and `confidenceScore`
- `Parse AI Analysis` validates JSON, falls back gracefully if vision confidence is low
- `Determine Severity` maps AI keywords (`crash`, `500`, `null`, `payment`, `data loss`) to `critical | high | medium | low` — overrides AI suggestion if hard keywords are detected

## 🎫 Platform Routing — Zendesk & Jira
**Nodes:** Route by Platform → Zendesk Add Comment → Jira Add Comment → Jira Attach File

- Switch node routes on `platform` field (`zendesk` or `jira`) — add more outputs for Linear, GitHub Issues, Freshdesk, etc.
- **Zendesk:** Posts a rich internal note with inline image embed (`![screenshot](url)`), full AI analysis table, severity badge, and a collapsible developer notes section. Also updates ticket tags with `visual-proof` and the severity label
- **Jira:** Adds a formatted comment in Jira wiki markup with the CDN image URL, AI breakdown, and affected component. A second API call attaches the raw file to the issue for download

## 📣 Severity Escalation & Response
**Nodes:** IF Critical/High? → Slack Alert → Build Final Response → Respond to Webhook

- IF node checks severity — only `critical` and `high` tickets trigger the Slack alert
- Slack message includes: ticket ID, customer name, product area, AI error summary, severity badge, CDN image link, and a direct link to the ticket
- `Build Final Response` assembles the full enriched summary: ticket URL, CDN URL, AI analysis, severity, detected keywords, and agent metadata
- Returns `200 OK` with the complete ticket enrichment record — ready to log or forward to a dashboard

nodes.name: GPT-4o Vision - Analyse Screenshot
nodes.notes: Passes the CDN URL to GPT-4o Vision. Returns structured error analysis: visible error message, affected component, browser/OS, developer notes, suggested priority, and keywords.

values.content: You are a senior software QA engineer and technical support specialist. Analyse the provided screenshot or screen recording URL and produce a structured developer-ready bug report. Return ONLY valid JSON — no markdown, no preamble.

nodes.name: Zendesk - Add Internal Note
nodes.notes: Posts a rich internal note with inline image embed, AI analysis table, developer notes, and reproducibility hints. Tags ticket with severity and visual-proof labels.

nodes.name: Jira - Update Issue Labels
nodes.notes: Updates Jira issue with AI-generated summary and labels before adding the comment.

nodes.name: Jira - Add Comment
nodes.notes: Adds a Jira wiki-markup formatted comment with image thumbnail, AI analysis table, developer notes, and reproducibility hints.

nodes.name: Slack - Escalate to Dev Channel
nodes.notes: Only fires for critical and high severity tickets when notifyDev is true. Posts to the configured dev channel with full error context, CDN image link, and direct ticket URL.

parameters.jsCode:
const slackResp = $input.first();
const data = $('Merge Platform Response').first().json;

return [{
  json: {
    success: true,
    message: `Visual proof attached to ${data.platform} ticket ${data.ticketId} with severity ${data.severity}.`,
    // Ticket
    platform: data.platform,
    ticketId: data.ticketId,
    ticketUrl: data.ticketUrl,
    platformCommentId: data.platformCommentId,
    // Asset
    cdnUrl: data.cdnUrl,
    structuredFilename: data.structuredFilename,
    fileSizeBytes: data.fileSizeBytes,
    isScreenRecording: data.isScreenRecording,
    // AI Analysis
    severity: data.severity,
    severityBadge: data.severityBadge,
    errorSummary: data.errorSummary,
    visibleErrorMessage: data.visibleErrorMessage,
    errorCode: data.errorCode,
    affectedComponent: data.affectedComponent,
    affectedUrl: data.affectedUrl,
    browserOrOS: data.browserOrOS,
    developerNotes: data.developerNotes,
    detectedKeywords: data.detectedKeywords,
    suggestedLabels: data.suggestedLabels,
    confidenceScore: data.confidenceScore,
    // Meta
    agentName: data.agentName,
    customerName: data.customerName,
    customerEmail: data.customerEmail,
    devNotified: data.notifyDev && ['critical', 'high'].includes(data.severity),
    submittedAt: data.submittedAt,
    processedAt: new Date().toISOString()
  }
}];
